log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, likely a Windows binary, with a size of 253961 bytes. It has been analyzed in multiple sandboxes, including CAPA, SNDBOX, Tencent HABO, VirusTotal Cuckoofork, and Viru
The sample has been observed to interact with 3 unique IPs, 6 unique domains, and 7 HTTP conversations. It has also dropped 2 files and created 3 processes. Additionally, it has created 0 mutexes and

The sample exhibits various behaviors, including calculating modulo 256, delaying execution, creating or opening files, accessing the Windows event log, encrypting data using RC4, and referencing anal
The sample has been observed to communicate with multiple IP addresses and domains, including 185.103.243.59, 66.171.248.178, and ipv4bot.whatismyipaddress.com. It has also made GET and POST requests

The sample has been observed to create two persistence keys in the Windows registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce\qgodgzurytb and \REGISTRY\MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows
The sample has been observed to drop two files: dqbing.exe and fulehj.exe. These files are likely used by the sample for its malicious activities.

Reviewer feedback: revisit these behavior phrases that are evidenced in the report.
They are behavior descriptions, not ATT&CK IDs. Map them only if you know the real MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise technique ID.

Instructions:

- Do not invent placeholder IDs such as T1234.
